context: This policy applies to Total Joint Replacement, Unicompartmental Replacement, and Patella-Femoral Arthroplasty.

Knee Arthroplasty is a surgery that is performed primarily for severe arthritis (osteoarthritis, inflammatory arthritides including rheumatoid arthritis, avascular necrosis and post-traumatic arthritis) of the knee joint.

subcriteria.criteria: Specific Indications: Knee Arthroplasty
context: Knee arthroplasty is medically necessary when all of the following indications (1-4) are met.

subcriteria.criteria: Condition
context: The patient must have one of the following conditions.

subcriteria.criteria: Near end-stage to end-stage osteoarthritis (primary or secondary) (e.g., near or complete loss of joint space, bone-on-bone with or without varus, or valgus malalignment)
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have near end-stage to end-stage osteoarthritis (primary or secondary)?

subcriteria.criteria: Inflammatory arthritidies including rheumatoid arthritis
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have inflammatory arthritidies including rheumatoid arthritis?

subcriteria.criteria: Osteonecrosis/ avascular necrosis (tibial plateau/femoral condyle) or post-traumatic arthritis
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have osteonecrosis/avascular necrosis or post-traumatic arthritis?

subcriteria.criteria: Persistent knee pain
context: Persistent knee pain with all of the following:

subcriteria.criteria: Significant pain that interferes with activities of daily living
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have significant pain that interferes with activities of daily living (ADLs)?

subcriteria.criteria: Significant pain that increases with weight bearing and/or standing
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have significant pain that increases with weight bearing and/or standing?

subcriteria.criteria: Significant pain with active or passive range of motion
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have significant pain with active or passive range of motion?

subcriteria.criteria: Conservative Management Requirement or Exclusion
context: Symptoms continue after conservative management OR the patient meets an exclusion to the requirement.

Conservative Management is defined as: A trial of oral analgesics (not to include opioids) and/or anti-inflammatory medications, if not contraindicated, and either one of the following has occurred: A trial of ambulatory support (e.g., use of a cane, walker, etc.) and patient education on joint protection and self-management with assistive devices; or Participation in a home-directed exercise program (preferred) or physical therapy; or If patient is unable to participate in the above program, or there are reasons for not delaying, documentation must be provided in the medical record.

subcriteria.criteria: Symptoms continue after conservative management
context: See definition of Conservative Management in parent context.
subcriteria.query: Do symptoms continue after a trial of conservative management (oral analgesics/anti-inflammatories AND either ambulatory support/education OR home exercise/PT)?

subcriteria.criteria: Exclusion to Conservative Management: Knee fracture with severe underlying osteoarthritis
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have a knee fracture with severe underlying osteoarthritis?

subcriteria.criteria: Exclusion to Conservative Management: Rapid progression of symptoms with severe disability and progressive radiologic deterioration
subcriteria.query: Is there rapid progression of symptoms with severe disability and progressive radiologic deterioration?

subcriteria.criteria: Exclusion to Conservative Management: Bone tumor involving the knee
subcriteria.query: Does the patient have a bone tumor involving the knee?

subcriteria.criteria: Exclusion to Conservative Management: Complete loss of joint space or bone-on-bone contact
subcriteria.query: Is there complete loss of joint space or bone-on-bone contact?

subcriteria.criteria: Exclusion to Conservative Management: Other reasons documented in the note
context: Including but not limited to, bilateral or multiple joint disease.
subcriteria.query: Are there other documented reasons to exclude conservative management (e.g., bilateral or multiple joint disease)?

subcriteria.criteria: Radiological evidence
context: Radiological evidence of end stage joint disease (bone-on-bone articulation) in one to three compartments, or near-end stage joint disease with joint space narrowing in one to three compartments with at least two of the following.

subcriteria.criteria: End stage joint disease (bone-on-bone articulation) in one to three compartments
subcriteria.query: Is there radiological evidence of end stage joint disease (bone-on-bone articulation) in one to three compartments?

subcriteria.criteria: Near-end stage joint disease with joint space narrowing in one to three compartments with at least two additional findings
Near-end stage joint disease with joint space narrowing in one to three compartments
subcriteria.query: Is there radiological evidence of near-end stage joint disease with joint space narrowing in one to three compartments?

subcriteria.criteria: At least two of the following findings
Malalignment
Joint subluxation
Subchondral cyst(s)
